description: PRD-8 Members UX cleanup: expand the role enum to the 6 human roles from docs/architecture.md, consolidate role list + permission gates into pure modules on both FE and BE, add project switcher + Name column to members page, add inline Manage Team action to /projects rows, delete the redundant /organization route. See GitHub issue #31.

userStories.title: DB migration — extend memberships.role to permit 4 new values
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the memberships.role constraint to accept the new role values so that downstream code can insert them without a CHECK violation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a new migration in migrations/ that extends the memberships.role CHECK constraint (or enum, whichever the current schema uses) to permit values: OCA, CM, cx_engineer, field_technician, design_engineer, owner_fm
Existing rows with role 'OCA' or 'cx_engineer' are not modified
Migration applies cleanly against a fresh DB and is idempotent on re-apply
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend roles module with is_valid_role + unit tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need one Python source of truth for valid role values so that the invite validator and any future gate cannot drift from the DB.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/roles.py exporting ROLES (the 6 canonical string values) and is_valid_role(s: str) -> bool
Display labels are exported as ROLE_LABELS dict keyed by role value
Create backend/tests/test_roles.py with cases: is_valid_role returns True for each of the 6 values, False for '', None, 'ADMIN', and case-variants like 'oca'
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend permissions module with can_manage_team / can_create_project + unit tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need named predicates for permission gates so that scattered role-string checks in the backend can be consolidated.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/permissions.py exporting can_manage_team(role) and can_create_project(role), both returning True for 'OCA' and 'CM', False otherwise
Both predicates return False for None, empty string, and unknown roles
Create backend/tests/test_permissions.py covering all 6 roles plus None/empty/unknown for both predicates
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Backend invite endpoint uses roles + permissions modules
userStories.description: As an OCA or CM, I want the invite endpoint to accept any of the 6 role values and to permit CM (not only OCA) to invite, so that the expanded role model is honored end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: InvitationService (or the FastAPI route in main.py) validates the incoming role via is_valid_role and rejects with a 400 if invalid
The 'Only OCAs can invite' 403 gate is replaced by a can_manage_team(caller_role) check, so CM is also permitted
Extend backend/tests/test_invites_endpoint.py with a case that POSTs role='CM' and asserts 200, and one that POSTs role='not_a_role' and asserts 400
Existing invite tests continue to pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend lib/roles module with isValidRole + unit tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need one TypeScript source of truth for valid role values so that role dropdowns and type unions stop being duplicated across files.
acceptanceCriteria: Create frontend/src/lib/roles.ts exporting ROLES (readonly tuple of the 6 values), Role type (union derived from ROLES), ROLE_LABELS (Record<Role, string>), and isValidRole(s: unknown): s is Role
Create frontend/src/lib/__tests__/roles.test.ts with cases: isValidRole returns true for each of the 6 values, false for '', null, undefined, 'ADMIN', and case-variants
Tests pass under vitest
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Frontend lib/permissions module with canManageTeam / canCreateProject + unit tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need named predicates so that scattered role === 'OCA' checks across the frontend can be consolidated.
acceptanceCriteria: Create frontend/src/lib/permissions.ts exporting canManageTeam(role: Role | null | undefined): boolean and canCreateProject(role: Role | null | undefined): boolean, both returning true only for 'OCA' and 'CM'
Create frontend/src/lib/__tests__/permissions.test.ts covering all 6 roles plus null/undefined for both predicates
Tests pass under vitest
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Widen role type unions in supabase.ts, getPrimaryOrg, members.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the existing 'OCA' | 'cx_engineer' literal unions widened to the full Role type, so that the 6-role model flows through type signatures without manual re-typing.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/lib/supabase.ts: change the membership role type alias to the imported Role from lib/roles
frontend/src/lib/getPrimaryOrg.ts: change the return-type role field to Role and the cast on firstMembership.role to Role
frontend/src/lib/members.ts: change getCurrentUserRole return type from 'OCA' | 'cx_engineer' | null to Role | null and update the cast
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Members page — expand Role dropdown to 6 values
userStories.description: As an OCA or CM, I want to pick from all 6 role values when inviting a new member so that I can assign Construction Manager, Field Technician, Design Engineer, and Owner/FM in addition to OCA and Cx Engineer.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/app/(app)/project/[id]/members/page.tsx: replace the hardcoded <option> elements with options generated by mapping over ROLES from lib/roles, using ROLE_LABELS for the visible text
Default formData.role remains 'cx_engineer'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Replace OCA-only gates with canManageTeam on members page
userStories.description: As a CM, I want to see the invite form and resend buttons on the members page, so that I can manage my team without OCA bottleneck.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/app/(app)/project/[id]/members/page.tsx: replace setIsOCA(role === 'OCA') and the isOCA state with a canManage boolean computed via canManageTeam(role)
All UI gates that referenced isOCA (invite form, resend button column) now reference canManage
Tests in frontend/src/lib/__tests__/members.test.ts (if any reference the role-gate shape) continue to pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Replace OCA gates on project and entity pages with canManageTeam / canCreateProject
userStories.description: As a CM, I want the project and entity pages to expose admin actions to me, so that capability matches the documented CM role.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/app/(app)/project/[id]/page.tsx: replace the two userRole === 'OCA' gates with canManageTeam(userRole) and widen useState type to Role | null
frontend/src/app/(app)/entity/[id]/page.tsx: replace the two user.role !== 'OCA' / user?.role === 'OCA' gates with calls to canManageTeam(user?.role); widen the role type on the user shape to Role
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Members page — add Name column to Current Members table
userStories.description: As any user, I want to see member names alongside emails so that I can identify teammates by name.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/app/(app)/project/[id]/members/page.tsx: prepend a 'Name' <th> before Email in the Current Members table header
Each row renders member.full_name as the first <td>, falling back to '—' when full_name is null/undefined/empty
Pending Invitations table is not modified
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Members page — add project switcher at top
userStories.description: As an OCA or CM, I want to switch between my projects' membership pages without navigating back to /projects so that managing multiple projects is fast.
acceptanceCriteria: Create frontend/src/components/ProjectSwitcher.tsx: a client component that fetches the current user's projects (via the participations + projects join already used in lib/members.ts patterns) and renders a <select> with the current projectId selected
onChange calls router.push(`/project/${newId}/members`)
Mount ProjectSwitcher at the top of frontend/src/app/(app)/project/[id]/members/page.tsx, above the H1 or directly under it
Picker hides itself (renders null) if the user has fewer than 2 projects
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Projects page — add inline Manage Team action per project row
userStories.description: As an OCA or CM, I want a Manage Team button on each project row in /projects, so that I can jump straight to membership management without entering the project first.
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/app/(app)/projects/page.tsx (or whichever component renders the projects list): add a 'Manage Team' button/link per row linking to /project/<id>/members
The action is rendered only when canManageTeam(currentUserRole) returns true
Currently-rendered project info (name, etc.) is preserved
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Delete /organization route and remove Organization link from UserMenu
userStories.description: As an end-user, I want the redundant /organization page gone so that /projects is the single canonical home for my project list and the user dropdown stops linking to a broken page.
acceptanceCriteria: Delete frontend/src/app/(app)/organization/page.tsx and the organization directory
Remove the <Link href="/organization"> Organization item from frontend/src/components/UserMenu.tsx (including its surrounding icon/markup)
Navigating to /organization returns 404 (or Next.js's default not-found behavior)
User dropdown no longer shows an Organization entry between Dark mode and Manage Team
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
